steps.thought: Sheet A is dated 3/25/26 = 2026. Plot 28, Saguaro No. 2A. 7 arms. Let me read sheet B.

Sheet B is dated 3-30-23 (2023), recorder Marcie, 5 arms. Sheet A is 2026, recorder Bri, 7 arms. So 2023 = Sheet B (5 arms, canonical numbers 1-5). 2026 = Sheet A (7 arms, must match to canonical). Let me lay out the data: **2023 (Sheet B) - canonical arm numbers:** - Arm 1: dir 31, A 5.48, B 1.01, C 5.60, D 1.03, E .2 - Arm 2: dir 124, A 4.58, B 1.03, C 5.00, D 1.02, E .6 - Arm 3: dir 163, A 3.32, B 1.02, C 3.71, D 1.01, E .4 - Arm 4: dir 224, A 2.67, B 1.06, C 4.08, D 1.06, E .8, note "failed nubbin beneath arm"

Let me first reason about direction matching: 2023 directions: 31, 124, 163, 224, 339 2026 directions: 36, 78, 137, 169, 193, 216, 345 Matching by closest direction: - 2023 arm1 (31) ↔ 2026 arm1 (36) → canonical 1 - 2023 arm2 (124) ↔ 2026 arm3 (137) → canonical 2 - 2023 arm3 (163) ↔ 2026 arm4 (169) → canonical 3 - 2023 arm4 (224) ↔ 2026 arm6 (216) → canonical 4 - 2023 arm5 (339) ↔ 2026 arm7 (345) → canonical 5 New 2026 arms: arm2 (78), arm5 (193). These are new arms with no canonical match (new since 2023). I need
This shows the saguaro 2A with people measuring. Let me look at more photos to understand the arm structure.
This is a clear photo of the full saguaro 2A. I can see arms emerging. Let me look at the other photos.

This view shows the saguaro from another angle. I can see multiple arms including a cluster low on the left side, a tall central stem, and arms on the right. Let me look at the 2023 photos to compare.
This 2023 photo (labeled "2") shows the saguaro. I can see a big arm curving up on the right, a smaller arm on the left near top, and lower arms. Let me view the remaining 2023 photos.
Now I have a good understanding. Let me re-examine the datasheets closely to get precise values. Let me re-view sheet B (2023).

2023 confirmed. Plot 28, Saguaro 2A. Date 3-30-23. 5 arms. The note for arm 4 is "failed nubbin beneath arm". The 2023 datum: B and D are ~1.0, meaning the "1 meter mark" was set at ground level basically (height to 1m mark ≈ 1.0). Good, datum is at ground. Now let me re-view sheet A (2026) closely.

Now I have all data clearly. Let me analyze the datum issue for 2026. The note at top-left of 2026: "7.62 + .46 = 8.08" — this is the Saguaro height calculation. The recorder Bri set datum differently. Most arms have B/D ≈ 1.0 (datum at ground, mark at 1.0m). But arm 2 (78°): A=-.47, B=-0.52, C=.61, D=-.42, E=.10, note "below bungee". The values are negative. The "below bungee" indicates the velcro/datum tape was placed differently here. The instruction notes: "Make sure the bottom of the Velcro tape is at 1.0 mete

The instruction says 2023 paper arm numbers become canonical, and the 2026 sheet must be matched back to those. I’m going to inspect the actual image files now so the final row numbering follows that rule rather than the later sheet’s numbering.

Sheet A is 2024-style date but the instruction says the pair represents the earlier survey versus 2026; Sheet B is clearly dated 3-7-26. I’m using Sheet A as the canonical-number sheet and checking photos for whether the 2026 “extra” nubbing is a real new canonical arm.
I’m going to crop the bottom of the 2026 sheet into a temporary image so the off-table nubbing row is easier to read. This won’t touch `submission.json`.
`PIL` isn’t usable in this sandbox because its binary wheel is the wrong architecture, so I’m using `sips`’ offset crop instead.
The offset direction is inverted from what I expected; I’ll flip it and inspect the lower row.
The 2026 table itself confirms rows 1-7; the added row is below the printed grid, so I’m shifting the crop further down to capture the handwritten continuation.
The continuation row is lower still; one more lower crop should capture it.
Sheet A: dated 4/5/24, plot 28, #112, Arm# 7, height 6.94, diameter .399. So this is likely the 2023 sheet (dated 2024 but covers 2023 measurements?). Let me read sheet B.

Now I have both sheets. Let me organize: **Sheet A** (dated 4/5/24, but covers earlier survey). Wait — sheet B is dated 3-7-26 (2026). Sheet A is dated 4/5/24. The task says one is 2023, one is 2026. Sheet A says "4/5/24" which is 2024, not 2023. But task says biologists measured in 2023 and 2026. The 2023 sheet must be Sheet A. Note: the date written might be when transcribed, but the task says "2023" sheet. Let me treat Sheet A = 2023 (the earlier one), Sheet B = 2026. Plot 28, Saguaro #112. saguaro_id = "28-112"
This confirms saguaro #112. Let me look at more photos to disambiguate arms.
This shows the saguaro from a different angle. I can see multiple arms. Let me look at remaining photos to understand the arm arrangement.

Now I can see the arm structure clearly. There's a cluster of arms around the lower-middle (multiple arms emerging close together - these are the ~130° arms and the "hub"). Let me check photo 2023_04 and the 2026 photos.
Now the 2026 photos.
Same saguaro in 2026. Let me view the remaining 2026 photos.
This 2026 view shows the arm cluster. I can see the multiple arms emerging from the hub area. Let me check 2026_03 and 2026_04 for the new nubbin arm.
Sheet A confirmed (2023). Now let me re-examine sheet B carefully.
